enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that Audiobooks.com is a scam because it charges $14/month for access to audiobooks that are freely available on LibriVox, which uses volunteer readers. He also criticizes Apple for technical restrictions that prevent smoothly playing sequential audio files and for phone behaviors (screen-on requirement, call interruptions) that make free alternatives harder to use, suggesting these are coordinated efforts to force paid services on consumers.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: It's a scam — profiting off volunteer labor and freely available public domain content
The correct free alternative; people should use it directly
Deliberately designed to benefit paid services, not a legitimate copyright concern
Charging for things that are essentially free to distribute is wrong
Good economies maximize free access to resources; unnecessary middlemen are harmful
Best system blends both — free resources with capitalist self-reliance, not rent-seeking gatekeepers
Skeptical — questions whether mandatory schooling exists to protect teacher employment rather than educate
People should be reading a book a day; one book per month is insulting
